message:
Add get_memory_usage to GraphemePool API

Implement get_memory_usage() method that estimates total memory used by
the pool in bytes, including:
- Slots vector (stack + heap)
- String heap allocations for each grapheme
- Free list vector
- Deduplication index HashMap (estimated)

This is useful for monitoring pool resource consumption and making
decisions about when to compact or resize.

Closes: bd-1y3z

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1xsw","title":"opentui terminal: improve capability detection heuristics (hyperlinks/sync output)","description":"# OpenTUI Terminal — Improve Capability Detection Heuristics (Hyperlinks + Sync Output)

## Problem

`Capabilities::detect()` currently enables `hyperlinks` and `sync_output` primarily based on `TERM_PROGRAM`.

In many real environments (and especially in PTY-based tests), `TERM_PROGRAM` is unset even when the terminal *does* support these features.

This creates false negatives that:

- make the demo_showcase less impressive by default
- make capability gating/inspector output misleading
- make PTY E2E assertions flaky unless the harness sets non-standard env vars

## Goal

Make capability detection robust using multiple signals:

- `TERM` value (e.g., `xterm-kitty`, `wezterm`, `ghostty`, `alacritty`)
- known terminal-specific env vars (e.g., `KITTY_WINDOW_ID`)
- `TERM_PROGRAM` (still supported)

…while remaining conservative for unknown terminals.

## Proposed Implementation

Refactor hyperlink/sync detection to consider:

- `TERM_PROGRAM` contains: WezTerm, Alacritty, kitty, ghostty, iTerm.app, Apple_Terminal, Hyper
- `TERM` contains: kitty, ghostty, wezterm, alacritty
- `KITTY_WINDOW_ID` present -> kitty features supported (at minimum: hyperlinks + sync output)

This can be implemented as:

- update `detect_hyperlinks(...)` and `detect_sync(...)` to accept `(term, term_program, kitty_window_id_present)`
- keep behavior explicit and testable (no hidden global env reads inside helpers)

## Tests (Required)

Add unit tests in `src/terminal/capabilities.rs` (or a dedicated test module) covering:

- hyperlinks true when TERM_PROGRAM indicates a supported terminal
- hyperlinks true when TERM indicates kitty/ghostty/alacritty/wezterm
- hyperlinks true when KITTY_WINDOW_ID is present
- sync_output true for kitty/alacritty/wezterm/ghostty via TERM or TERM_PROGRAM
- both false for unknown terminal strings

## Why This Matters for demo_showcase

- `bd-1ob` capability gating should be accurate.
- `bd-1t2` inspector should report real capability state.
- PTY E2E (`bd-61g0`) becomes more stable without relying on harness env hacks.","acceptance_criteria":"Done when:
- `Capabilities::detect()` enables `hyperlinks` and `sync_output` on known terminals even when `TERM_PROGRAM` is unset (using TERM and/or KITTY_WINDOW_ID).
- Detection remains conservative for unknown terminals (does not blindly enable).
- Unit tests cover the detection matrix (TERM/TERM_PROGRAM/KITTY_WINDOW_ID combinations) and pass under `cargo test`.
- demo_showcase capability gating (`bd-1ob`) and inspector (`bd-1t2`) can rely on these flags being accurate by default.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:31:59.374390069Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T03:00:28.996476468Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T03:00:28.996408882Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["capabilities","demo_showcase","terminal"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1xsw","depends_on_id":"bd-29z","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:33:40.731678966Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1xvx","title":"Implement move_to_word_boundary(direction, type) - Word boundary navigation","description":"# Task: Implement move_to_word_boundary()

## Function Signature (Rust)
```rust
pub fn move_to_word_boundary(&mut self, direction: Direction, boundary: BoundaryType)
```

## Types
```rust
pub enum Direction { Forward, Backward }
pub enum BoundaryType { Start, End }
```

## Purpose
Moves cursor to the specified word boundary type in the given direction. More flexible than simple word movement.

## Implementation Notes
- Forward + Start: next word start
- Forward + End: current/next word end
- Backward + Start: previous word start
- Backward + End: previous word end

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Direction enum defined
- [ ] BoundaryType enum defined
- [ ] All 4 combinations work
- [ ] Works with Unicode

## Testing
- All direction/boundary combinations
- At document boundaries","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:30:07.226055808Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T15:30:07.241444652Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1xvx","depends_on_id":"bd-2dyx","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:30:07.241398676Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1y3z","title":"Implement get_memory_usage() - Pool memory usage","description":"# Task: Implement get_memory_usage()

## Function Signature (Rust)
```rust
pub fn get_memory_usage(&self) -> usize
```

## Purpose
Returns total memory used by the pool in bytes. Includes string storage and metadata.

## Implementation Notes
- Sum of all string lengths
- Plus Vec overhead
- Plus ref_count storage
- Consider alignment

## Calculation
```rust
// Rough calculation
let strings_size: usize = self.strings.iter()
    .filter_map(|s| s.as_ref())
    .map(|s| s.len() + std::mem::size_of::<String>())
    .sum();
let metadata_size = self.ref_counts.len() * std::mem::size_of::<u32>();
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Returns reasonable byte count
- [ ] Includes all allocations

## Testing
- Empty pool
- Pool with various string sizes","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:35:44.481470186Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T15:35:44.487126032Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1y3z","depends_on_id":"bd-g3az","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:35:44.487098511Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1ysr","title":"Add gamma-correct color averaging in PixelBuffer","description":"## Problem

src/buffer/pixel.rs lines 339-385 (average_colors function) performs linear averaging in sRGB space:

```rust
fg_r += color.r;  // Accumulates sRGB values directly
// ...
Rgba::rgb(fg_r / count, fg_g / count, fg_b / count)  // Averages in sRGB
```

## Why This Is Wrong

sRGB is NOT linear. Averaging in sRGB space causes **darkening bias**:

- Red (1.0, 0.0, 0.0) + Blue (0.0, 0.0, 1.0)
- Current: (0.5, 0.0, 0.5) - appears ~30% darker than correct
- Correct: Convert to linear, average, convert back

## Impact

- Pixel supersampling produces darker-than-expected colors
- Gradients look washed out
- Color transitions have incorrect midpoints

## Required Fix

Add sRGB ↔ linear conversion functions:

```rust
fn srgb_to_linear(value: f32) -> f32 {
    if value <= 0.04045 {
        value / 12.92
    } else {
        ((value + 0.055) / 1.055).powf(2.4)
    }
}

fn linear_to_srgb(value: f32) -> f32 {
    if value <= 0.0031308 {
        value * 12.92
    } else {
        1.055 * value.powf(1.0 / 2.4) - 0.055
    }
}
```

Then update average_colors():

```rust
fn average_colors(colors: &[Rgba], mask: &[bool]) -> (Rgba, Rgba) {
    // Convert to linear, accumulate, convert back
    let mut lin_r = 0.0f32;
    for (color, &active) in colors.iter().zip(mask) {
        if active {
            lin_r += srgb_to_linear(color.r);
            // ...
        }
    }
    let avg_r = linear_to_srgb(lin_r / count);
    // ...
}
```

## Scope Decision

This is a **significant change** that affects visual output. Consider:
1. Adding feature flag for gamma-correct mode
2. Documenting the change in CHANGELOG
3. Visual comparison tests before/after

## Priority

P2 - Improves visual quality but existing behavior is 'acceptable' for many use cases","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-28T23:05:21.216298314Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T00:20:12.990222170Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T00:20:12.990158351Z","close_reason":"Implemented gamma-correct color averaging in PixelBuffer. Added srgb_to_linear and linear_to_srgb conversion functions. Modified average_colors to accumulate in linear space and convert back. Added 5 tests verifying correctness.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ysr","depends_on_id":"bd-affo","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T23:05:21.235791552Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1z1r","title":"E2E: PTY Test CI Integration","description":"Enable PTY tests in CI with proper environment setup.

## CI Configuration
1. Remove `#[ignore]` or make conditional on CI env
2. Add pty-tests job to .github/workflows/check.yml
3. Configure proper PTY allocation in CI runner
4. Set appropriate timeouts (2-5 min per test)
5. Upload artifacts on failure

## Environment Setup
```yaml
env:
  TERM: xterm-256color
  HARNESS_ARTIFACTS: 1
  RUST_BACKTRACE: 1
```

## Conditional Ignore
```rust
#[cfg_attr(not(feature = "pty-tests"), ignore = "..." )]
```

## Dependencies

PTY tests run with continue-on-error for CI stability while still collecting artifacts.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1z1r","depends_on_id":"bd-1ci7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:49:01.090845208Z","created_by":"claude"},{"issue_id":"bd-1z1r","depends_on_id":"bd-397z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:49:00.367068951Z","created_by":"claude"},{"issue_id":"bd-1z1r","depends_on_id":"bd-4cr6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:48:38.418360642Z","created_by":"claude"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1z6r","title":"Implement select_word() - Select word at cursor","description":"# Task: Implement select_word()

3. Set selection to (start, end)
